items.title: Tweed Fisheries Amendment Act 1859
items.description: An Act to amend “TheTweedFisheries Act 1857,” and to alter the Annual Close Times in the River Tweed.

items.title: Burial Act 1859
items.description: An Act more effectively to prevent Danger to the Public Health from Places of Burial.

items.title: An Act to settle the Form of Affirmation to be made in certain Cases by Quakers and other Persons by Law permitted to make an Affirmation instead of taking an Oath.
The East India Loan Act 1859
Defence Act 1859
items.description: An Act to make further provision for the purchase of common and other rights by Her Majesty’s Principal Secretary of State for the War Department, and in relation to land vested in or taken by such Secretary of State.

items.title: An Act to amend the Law concerning Patents for Inventions with respect to Inventions for Improvements in Instruments and Munitions of War.
The Manor Courts Abolition (Ireland) Act 1859
An Act to indemnify such Persons in the United Kingdom as have omitted to qualify themselves for Offices and Employments, and to extend the Time limited for those Purposes respectively.

An Act to enable the Judges to appoint Commissioners within Ten Miles of London and in the Isle of Man and the Channel Islands to administer Oaths in Common Law, and to authorize the taking in the Country of Bail in Error, and Recognizances and Bail on the Revenue Side of the Exchequer.
An Act to continue an Act of the Eleventh and Twelfth Years of Her present Majesty, for amending the Laws relating to Savings Banks in Ireland.
An Act for amending and confirming a Scheme of the Charity Commissioners for Sir Thomas White's Charity and the Free Grammar School in the Town of Nottingham.
The Public Offices Extension Act 1859
An Act to repeal certain Acts and Parts of Acts which relate to the Observance of the Thirtieth of January and other Days.
The Evidence by Commission Act 1859
An Act to amend the Medical Act (1858)
An Act for raising the Sum of Thirteen million two hundred and seventy-seven thousand four hundred Pounds by Exchequer Bills, for the Service of the Year One thousand eight hundred and fifty-nine.
An Act to apply a Sum out of the Consolidated Fund to the Service of the Year One thousand eight hundred and fifty-nine, and to appropriate the Supplies granted in this Session of Parliament.
An Act to render valid certain Marriages in the Church of Saint James Baldersby in the County of York.
The Convict Prisons Abroad Act 1859
Expression The Superannuation Act 1859
The Recreation Grounds Act 1859
An Act to continue the Act for the Regulation of the Annuities and Premiums of the Naval Medical Supplemental Fund Society.
An Act to continue the Act for charging the Maintenance of certain Paupers upon the Union Funds.
The Annual Inclosure Act 1859
Confirmation and Probate Amendment Act 1859
items.description: An Act to amend the " Confirmation and Probate Act, 1858."

items.title: The Local Government Supplemental Act 1859
Remission of Penalties Act 1859
items.description: An Act to amend the Law concerning the Remission of Penalties.

items.title: An Act to enable Coroners in England to admit to Bail Persons charged with Manslaughter.
An Act to amend and explain an Act of the Sixth Year of the Reign of King George the Fourth, to repeal the Laws relating to the Combination of Workmen, and to make other Provisions in lieu thereof.
The Municipal Corporation Act 1859
The Probate Duty Act 1859
The Customs Amendment Act 1859
An Act further to amend the Laws relating to the Militia.
The East India Loan (No. 2) Act 1859
An Act for punishing Mutiny and Desertion, and for the better Payment of the Army and their Quarters.
The Royal Naval Reserve (Volunteer) Act 1859
The Government of India Act 1859
An Act to provide for the Establishment of a Reserve Force of Men who have been in Her Majesty's Service.
Inclosure Act 1859
items.description: An Act to amend and extend the Provisions of the Acts of the Inclosure, Exchange, and Improvement of Land.

items.title: An Act to continue the Act for the Exemption of Stock in Trade from Rating.
An Act to continue certain temporary Provisions concerning Ecclesiastical Jurisdiction in England.
Episcopal and Capitular Estates Act 1859 (repealed 5.11.1993)
items.description: An Act to continue and amend the Act concerning the Management of Episcopal and Capitular Estates in England.

items.title: The Second Annual Inclosure Act 1859
An Act to continue the Corrupt Practices Prevention Act, 1854.
The Poor Law (Payment of Debts) Act 1859
An Act for the Regulation of Her Majesty's Royal Marine Forces while on shore.
An Act further to continue the Exemption of certain Charities from the Operation of the Charitable Trusts Acts.
The Annual Turnpike Acts Continuance Act 1859
The Dublin Police Act 1859
The Savings Bank (Charitable Societies) Act 1859

An Act to defray the Charge of the Pay, Clothing, and contingent and other Expenses of the Disembodied Militia in Great Britain and Ireland; to grant Allowances in certain Cases to Subaltern Officers, Adjutants, Paymasters, Quartermasters, Surgeons, Assistant Surgeons, and Surgeons Mates of the Militia; and to authorize the Employment of the Non-commissioned Officers.

An Act to apply a Sum out of the Consolidated Fund and the Surplus of Ways and Means to the Service of the Year One thousand eight hundred and fifty-nine; and to appropriate the Supplies granted in this Session of Parliament.
An Act to amend the Act of the Fifth and Sixth Years of King William the Fourth, Chapter Sixty-three, relating to Weights and Measures.
An Act limiting the Power of Imprisonment for Small Debts exercised by the County Court Judges.
The Westminster Bridge Act 1859
Railway Companies Arbitration Act 1859

An Act to apply the Sum of One million two hundred twenty-two thousand three hundred and eighty-three Pounds Eight Shillings and Ninepence out of the Consolidated Fund to the Service of the Year ending the Thirty-first Day of March One thousand eight hundred and fifty-nine.
An Act to extend the Powers of an Act passed in the Thirteenth and Fourteenth Years of Her Majesty, Chapter One hundred and eleven, relating to the laying down of Railways at Holyhead Harbour.
The Matrimonial Causes Act 1859
An Act to amend the Irish Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act (1857).
British Law Ascertainment Act 1859
items.description: An Act to afford Facilities for the more certain Ascertainment of the Law administered in one Part of Her Majesty's Dominions when pleaded in the Courts of another Part thereof.

items.title: An Act to remove Doubts as to the Validity of certain Marriages of British Subjects at Lisbon.
The Petty Sessional Divisions Act 1859
The Sale of Gas Act 1859
An Act to apply the Sum of Eleven Millions out of the Consolidated Fund to the Service of the Year One thousand eight hundred and fifty-nine.
An Act to repeal the Thirty-second Section of the Act "for the more easy Recovery of Small Debts and Demands "in England," and to make further Provision in lieu thereof.
An Act to effect an Exchange of Ecclesiastical Patronage between Her Majesty the Queen and Miss Sophia Broadley.
